title: eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. ambiguum
intro: Eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. ambiguum, also known as the woodland lupine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is a perennial herb that contains compounds with potential medicinal properties, including alkaloids and glycosides. Research suggests that the plant may have health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to its bioactive compounds. Eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. ambiguum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
